message:
🌱 Add keycloak: Default values for User attributes. mission (#875)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-36160-default-values-for-user-attributes",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Default values for User attributes",
+    "description": "Default values for User attributes. Requested by 23+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current Keycloak setup",
+        "description": "Verify your Keycloak version supports default attribute values:\n```bash\n# If running in a container:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config\n\n# Check the container image version:\ndocker inspect keycloak | grep -i image\n```\nThis feature requires a Keycloak version with the fix from PR #39746 (check Keycloak release notes for availability)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the feature",
+        "description": "Previously, user attributes in Keycloak had no concept of default values. When a user was created without providing a value for a configured attribute, it was stored as null. This feature adds the ability to:\n\n1. Define default values for user attributes in the User Profile configuration\n2. Automatically assign the default value when no value is provided during user creation or registration\n3. Validate default values against the attribute's configured validators\n4. Pre-fill default values in the Admin Console, Registration Form, and other interfaces"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure default values for user attributes",
+        "description": "Set up default values in the Keycloak Admin Console:\n1. Go to **Realm Settings > User Profile**\n2. Select an existing attribute or create a new one\n3. In the attribute configuration, find the **Default Value** setting\n4. Enter the desired default value\n5. Save the configuration\n\nThe default value will be automatically applied whenever a user is created without an explicit value for that attribute. The default value is validated against any configured validators (e.g., length, regex patterns).\n\nSee the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/39746) for implementation details."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify default values work",
+        "description": "Test that default attribute values are applied correctly:\n1. Create a new user without setting the attribute that has a default value\n2. Check the user's attributes in the Admin Console or via the Admin REST API:\n```bash\ncurl -s -H \"Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN\" \\\n  https://your-keycloak/admin/realms/your-realm/users/$USER_ID \\\n  | jq '.attributes'\n```\n3. Confirm the attribute has the configured default value instead of null"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Keycloak added support for default values in user attributes. When configured in the User Profile settings, default values are automatically assigned to attributes that are not explicitly set during user creation or registration. Default values are validated against the attribute's configured validators and pre-filled in the Admin Console and Registration Form.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",
+      "identity",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36160",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/39746"
+    },
+    "reactions": 23,
+    "comments": 1,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "docker"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) with Admin Console access."
+  },
+  "security": {
